quotes.quote: Whether the code succeeds or fails, a self-reflection is produced based on the experimental results or the encountered error signal (Renze & Guven (2024); Shinn et al. (2024)). Here, the mle-solver is prompted to reflect on the outcome of its actions. If the program failed to compile, the solver reflects on how to fix this issue in next iterations. If it successfuly compiles and returns a score, the solver will reflect on how to increase this score.
quotes.rationale: Compile/run outcome and score drive iterative revision of the candidate code.

quotes.quote: This is facilitated by mle-solver, a specialized module designed to generate, test, and refine machine learning code autonomously. mle-solver begins by producing initial code based on the research plan and insights from the literature review.
quotes.rationale: Establishes the module implements generate-test-refine autonomously.

quotes.quote: Instead of using an LLM scoring function, the mle-solver score is evaluated on the dev set, which is a 20% random sample taken from the original training set, and the training set is represented by the other 80% split.
quotes.rationale: A held-out dev-set metric, not an LLM judge, supplies the scalar score guiding solver search.

quotes.quote: Based on these scores, the PhD agent then decides whether to finalize the project or revisit earlier subtasks—such as planning, experimentation, or results interpretation—to address the feedback. This allows the agents to refine the research report until it meets sufficiently high standards, effectively simulating the real-world academic revision process.
quotes.rationale: Scores route back into stage transition and revision decisions, not merely terminal output.

quotes.quote: In autonomous mode, there is no human involvement other than providing the initial research idea for agents to produce research for. Each subtask moves on to the next subtask sequentially upon completion.
quotes.rationale: In autonomous mode subtask advancement is unconditional on human gating; no validation criterion is reported for the transition.

quotes.quote: The paper-solver aims to act as a report generator, positioning the work that has been produced by previous stages of Agent Laboratory. paper-solver does not aim to entirely replace the academic paper-writing process, but rather to summarize the research that has been produced in a human-readable format so that the researcher using Agent Laboratory understands what has been accomplished.
quotes.rationale: The report is directed at a human researcher's comprehension, but no evaluative decision role or routing is specified.

quotes.quote: Unlike previous approaches, where agents participate in their own research ideation independent of human input (Baek et al. (2024); Lu et al. (2024b)), Agent Laboratory is designed to assist human scientists in executing their own research ideas using language agents. Agent Laboratory takes as input a human research idea and outputs a research report and code repository produced by autonomous language agents
quotes.rationale: The human supplies the idea and receives the report, establishing the non-independent assistive relationship.

diagnostics.consequence: A closed control loop over the whole research pipeline is driven by an unvalidated LLM score, so acceptance decisions cannot be treated as evidence of quality.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Based on these scores, the PhD agent then decides whether to finalize the project or revisit earlier subtasks—such as planning, experimentation, or results interpretation—to address the feedback. This allows the agents to refine the research report until it meets sufficiently high standards, effectively simulating the real-world academic revision process.

diagnostics.statement: No reliability study of any kind is reported for the PhD-agent report scoring channel that gates finalization versus revisiting earlier subtasks.

diagnostics.consequence: Errors produced in an early subtask can propagate downstream unchecked, since completion rather than correctness triggers the transition.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: In autonomous mode, there is no human involvement other than providing the initial research idea for agents to produce research for. Each subtask moves on to the next subtask sequentially upon completion.

diagnostics.statement: In autonomous mode subtasks advance sequentially upon completion with no reported validation criterion or human gate.

diagnostics.consequence: Execution-trace validation is scoped to individually supplied files, bounding the executability evidence to a narrower artifact than a working repository.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Agent Laboratory is also not able to manage repository-level code on its own, but rather the appropriate files are provided to it at each necessary step and files are saved based on which phase produced the file. Enabling flexible repository-level file modification and execution is a clear next step for future work.

diagnostics.statement: The system cannot manage repository-level code on its own; appropriate files must be supplied to it at each necessary step.

diagnostics.consequence: Reported task-performance gains may reflect adaptation to the dev split rather than generalizable empirical adequacy.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Instead of using an LLM scoring function, the mle-solver score is evaluated on the dev set, which is a 20% random sample taken from the original training set, and the training set is represented by the other 80% split.

diagnostics.statement: The mle-solver search signal is a 20% random dev split of the training set, with no reported analysis of overfitting or selection bias from repeated optimization against that split.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Based on these scores, the PhD agent then decides whether to finalize the project or revisit earlier subtasks—such as planning, experimentation, or results interpretation—to address the feedback. This allows the agents to refine the research report until it meets sufficiently high standards, effectively simulating the real-world academic revision process.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report a discrimination or agreement study for the PhD-agent scoring channel against independent judgments of the same reports, so the finalize-versus-revisit gate has documented reliability for the aggregate-quality property it controls.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Agreement statistic reported against a stated chance-agreement baseline
success_check.evidence_needed: A published agreement or discrimination study covering this exact scoring channel and its gating decisions
success_check.observable: Concordance between PhD-agent finalize/revise decisions and independent judgments on the same reports

exact_quotes.exact_quote: In autonomous mode, there is no human involvement other than providing the initial research idea for agents to produce research for. Each subtask moves on to the next subtask sequentially upon completion.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add and report an explicit correctness criterion that gates each autonomous-mode subtask transition instead of advancing on completion alone.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Downstream error rate lower than the ungated sequential baseline
success_check.evidence_needed: Execution traces of autonomous runs with and without the gate
success_check.observable: Documented per-subtask gating criterion and the rate of blocked transitions in autonomous runs

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Instead of using an LLM scoring function, the mle-solver score is evaluated on the dev set, which is a 20% random sample taken from the original training set, and the training set is represented by the other 80% split.

Agent Laboratory is also not able to manage repository-level code on its own, but rather the appropriate files are provided to it at each necessary step and files are saved based on which phase produced the file. Enabling flexible repository-level file modification and execution is a clear next step for future work.

recommendations.proposed_action: Evaluate mle-solver outcomes on a held-out split disjoint from the dev set used for search, and report the gap alongside the scope limits imposed by file-at-a-time rather than repository-level execution.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Held-out score within a pre-stated margin of the dev-set score
success_check.evidence_needed: Benchmark metrics reported on both splits across solver iterations
success_check.observable: Difference between the dev-set score used for search and a held-out score on the same tasks

memory_structure: research plan and literature-review insights carried into code generation
self-reflections on prior success or failure carried into next iterations
per-phase file saving keyed to the producing phase
policy_type: iterative self-reflection loop over code generation driven by compile status and dev-set score
agent-decided stage transition or revisit of earlier subtasks
sequential subtask progression in autonomous mode
human-supplied research idea as task specification
